items.title: Liverpool v Brighton: FA Cup fourth round – live
items.summary: Updates from 8pm kickoff (GMT) at Anfield Follow us over on Bluesky | And get in touch: email Scott 1 min: Whigfield lied about the air. It’s a cold night on Merseyside. After an exuberant rendition of You’ll Never Walk Alone, Brighton get the ball rolling. They’re kicking towards the Kop in this first half. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Taha opent score in de Euroborg, feest voor Liverpool in de FA Cup
items.summary: Zaterdag staan er vier Eredivisie-duels op het programma. Voor Ajax tegen Fortuna Sittard is er een apart liveblog, voor de rest kun je terecht in deze VI Live. En uiteraard ook voor het belangrijkste voetbal in het buitenland.

items.title: Klaassen dolblij met Ajax-Belgen: 'Lekker hè, zijn ze toch niet zo dom'
items.summary: Davy Klaassen hoopt dat de overwinning op Fortuna Sittard een voedingsbodem wordt voor meer. Dat zei de aanvoerder van Ajax vlak na de 4-1 zege van zaterdagavond.

items.title: 'The officials looked petrified' - was Villa Park chaos advert for VAR?
items.summary: Newcastle United beat Aston Villa in the FA Cup fourth round in a match dominated by a number of controversial decisions without VAR.

items.title: Belgen helpen Ajax voorbij Fortuna op vervelende avond voor Zinchenko
items.summary: Ajax heeft zaterdagavond uitstekende zaken gedaan in de strijd om de tweede plek in de Eredivisie. De Amsterdammers namen binnen een halfuur al ruim afstand van Fortuna Sittard, dat uiteindelijk vier Belgische treffers om de oren kreeg. Het uitvallen van Oleksandr Zinchenko was een smet op de zege voor Ajax.

items.title: Aston Villa latest: 'Referees need VAR,' says Emery after Newcastle loss
Fàbregas leest Morata de les na rood: 'Dan moet je een andere sport zoeken'
items.summary: Álvaro Morata pakte zaterdagmiddag een rode kaart bij Como en daar was Cesc Fàbregas niet over te spreken. De spits liet zich provoceren, maar volgens zijn trainer moet je daar als voetballer gewoon tegen kunnen.

items.title: Ajax bouwt weer op Farioli-fundament
items.summary: Een Italiaanse voetbalfan die na een jaar weer eens een wedstrijd van Ajax besloot op te zetten, had zomaar kunnen denken dat Francesco Farioli in de thuiswedstrijd tegen Fortuna Sittard nog aan het roer stond. Fred Grim kopieerde tegen Fortuna de aanvallende veldbezetting van de huidige Porto-trainer, nadat de interim-coach eerder defensief al was teruggekeerd naar de oude structuur .

items.title: King set for NWSL return after open-heart surgery
items.summary: Angel City FC defender Savy King has been removed from the National Women's Soccer League's season-ending injury list (SEI), the club announced on Saturday.

items.title: Third-tier Mansfield oust Burnley in FA Cup shock
items.summary: Burnley were on the wrong end of an FA Cup upset as they were beaten 2-1 by Mansfield on Saturday.

items.title: Tottenham appoint Igor Tudor as new head coach
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur have appointed Igor Tudor as their new head coach, the club have confirmed.

items.title: Ronaldo ends protest, scores 18 mins later
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo returned to Al Nassr's lineup for Saturday's match at Al Fateh -- and swiftly scored.

items.title: Pep not happy with Man City's 'boring' FA Cup win
items.summary: Pep Guardiola branded Manchester City's routine FA Cup victory over Salford City on Saturday as "boring".

items.title: Tonali inspires Newcastle to win over Villa in controversial tie
items.summary: Sandro Tonali scores twice as Newcastle United overcome multiple controversial refereeing decisions to knock 10-man Aston Villa out of the FA Cup.

items.title: Keith Andrews’s gravity-defying miracle has Brentford dreaming of Europe | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: Their best players and managers may move on, but this thoroughly modern club keep punching above their weight When the news cycle spins so fast, it’s worth remembering where Brentford were in the summer. They had lost their popular manager of seven years, Thomas Frank. They had lost their two best forwards, Bryan Mbeumo and Yoane Wissa. They had lost their goalkeeper Mark Flekken. And they had lost two stalwarts in Christian Nørgaard and Ben Mee (even if the latter’s involvement the previous season had been limited as he turned 35). Departure and replacement is an unavoidable part of life for a club such as Brentford, but this seemed a like a lot to deal with. Their summer signings were hard to judge. As a rule of thumb, if Brentford are signing someone about whom you already have considered opinions, it’s likely something has gone awry. That said, Caoimhín Kelleher’s gifts are clear, and a fee of just under £13m seemed good value for a goalkeeper with Premier League experience, while Dango Ouattara had demonstrated at Bournemouth how effective he could be either through the middle or out wide. But Antoni Milambo, Michael Kayode and Kaye Furo were unknown quantities. Continue reading...

items.title: Sandro Tonali fires Newcastle past 10-man Aston Villa on bad night for officials
items.summary: Football’s interminable video assistant refereeing debate has its latest chapter following a bizarre evening, and refereeing performance, at Villa Park. With the FA Cup not allowing the video protocol until the fifth round, this was an occasion to make the abolitionists think twice. Is elite football already too far gone to officiate without a bank of screens in a faraway business park? Or were Chris Kavanagh, a referee promoted to the Uefa elite list in December, and his assistants just having a nightmare day at the office? Sandro Tonali’s two goals before Nick Woltemade’s clincher booked Newcastle’s fifth round place, completing a comeback in the face of officiating mistakes weighing against Eddie Howe’s team. It was Aston Villa who lost their discipline. They should have been down to 10 men earlier than they eventually were. Continue reading...

items.title: HORROR HOLE! | McIlroy hits triple bogey at Pebble Beach
items.summary: Rory McIlroy drops three shots as he hits triple bogey at the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am.

items.title: Brilliant Mansfield pile pressure on Parker - is his time up at Burnley?
items.summary: Burnley manager Scott Parker is under increasing pressure as the Premier League side are dumped out of the FA Cup by League One Mansfield.

items.title: Forever Young superb once again in Saudi Cup
items.summary: Forever Young created history when becoming the first dual winner of the world’s most valuable horse race, the Saudi Cup.

items.title: From 'demoralising' 8-0 defeat to pushing Man City all the way
items.summary: Thirteen months after being embarrassed 8-0 by Manchester City, Salford City produce a performance to make their fans proud.
